narrative.summary: Thoughtful Japanese cooking in a charming room; 4.5 rating, good for a late weeknight bite.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're in Little Portugal, west along Dundas past the vintage shops and Portuguese cafés. Inside, it's a compact room with warm wood surfaces and low lighting—the kind of minimal Japanese aesthetic that feels deliberate but not sterile. A small bar anchors one side, a handful of tables fill the rest.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The menu skews izakaya-style—small plates meant for sharing, though the portions lean genuinely small. Tokyo toast arrives as thick-cut deep-fried bread topped with taro ice cream and crunchy toasted soba, a textural hit that justifies its cult following. The karaage ribs come tender and well-seasoned, while rotating specials like deep-fried octopus can be hit-or-miss. Shochu highballs pack serious punch, and cocktails like the Kuchibeni (vodka, calpico, strawberry) show more finesse than the food sometimes does.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: This is your late weeknight spot when you want a drink and a few bites without commitment. Come on a Tuesday or Wednesday, order the Tokyo toast and whatever sounds good, and don't expect to leave full.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Cool vibes & drinks with  SMALL plates.

Love the Tokyo toast ($10) deep fried thick toast with two scoops of taro ice cream and some crunchy toasted soba (feels like corn flakes).

Tried a few other small plates and I mean they’re SMALL small. Karachi ribs was tender and flavourful. Can’t remember the rest because they were quite forgettable honestly.
Unfortunately the rotational feature, a deep fried octopus, was kinda soggy and there wasn’t much flavour. The powder on the side also added absolutely nothing to the dish.

Drinks are quite good, I’d recommend the Kuchibeni ($17) with vodka, calico, and strawberry.
The Shochu Highballs were quite strong, the oolong one tastes like watery tea spiked with hard alcohol.

I think for 3 people, 3 drinks each, and 3 small dishes, total was about $250. Staff were friendly and attentive! Love the old school artsy Japanese hole in the wall wooden decor aesthetic. Fun place for  drinks and to soak up the vibes, and maybe be a bit more selective on the dishes."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Food 5/5
Service 4/5
Vibe 5/5

Another hidden Japanese style reso GEM in Toronto DT.

I have really high hopes as it was a MISSION to get a reservation, and they did not disappoint. 👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻

My personal fav was the toast dessert, liver paste with garlic toast and corns tempura. I feel a lot of dishes are a mix of Japanese and chinese culture (but not 100% sure), but everyone was a seller! Love, yum and want more b

Overall 5/5! definitely coming back and trying out more dishes! #eatwithclaw"

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Tapas style Japanese. The corn tempura, beef ribs, pork, sashimi, curry are all winners. Very strong dishes all around with small sharing portions. Exceptional atmosphere really took me back to a cozy Osaka establishment, full of 90s Japanese vinyl and trinkets. Recommended."
